perspectives.excerpt: World Cup 2026: Manzambi's brilliance guides Switzerland past Algeria Switzerland secured their passage to the last 16 with a disciplined 2-0 victory over Algeria in Vancouver, with goals from Breel Embolo and Dan Ndoye proving decisive. The Nati finally broke their curse in knockout football, while the Fennecs' tournament ended in disappointment after showing early promise.…

perspectives.headline: Switzerland beat Algeria 2-0 to reach World Cup Round of 16 in style

perspectives.headline: A Short Economic History of the United States
perspectives.excerpt: A Short Economic History of the United States From a slave economy to a global empire in 250 years. Welcome to FP Free Week. To celebrate America’s 250th birthday, our entire site is completely unlocked this week only. Enjoy the article.…

perspectives.headline: US history gets a presidential makeover for its 250th birthday
perspectives.excerpt: This year, the United States is marking 250 years of independence with celebrations across the country. An historic occasion that US President Donald Trump intends to turn into the crowning achievement of his second term by changing the appearance of Washington DC to his taste – and infusing his vision of American history into national museums, parks and monuments.…

stories.headline_en: Lagarde Won’t Exclude Early ECB Exit to Enter French Politics

perspectives.headline: Lagarde Won’t Exclude Early ECB Exit to Enter French Politics
perspectives.excerpt: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said she won’t rule out leaving her current position early to participate in French politics.

perspectives.headline: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics
perspectives.excerpt: The European Central Bank's Christine Lagarde has declined to rule out an early end to her term as president, as she mulls a foray into French politics. Lagarde, whose term as ECB President ends in October 2027, told French newspaper Les Echos an early departure is "possible" ahead of the country's presidential elections that year.…

perspectives.headline: France records 2,025 excess deaths at peak of heatwave as Europe braces for more extreme weather
perspectives.excerpt: France records 2,025 excess deaths at peak of heatwave as Europe braces for more extreme weather - Published France has said it recorded an increase of more than 2,000 deaths during the last week of a record-breaking European heatwave in June as forecasters warn of further extreme temperatures on the continent in the next few days.…

perspectives.headline: France records 2,025 excess deaths in June heatwave
perspectives.excerpt: France records 2,025 excess deaths in June heatwave France recorded 2,025 excess deaths during the June 22-28 heatwave, the country's health minister said Friday, marking a nearly 30 percent nationwide rise in recorded deaths. In the Paris region, the number of deaths during the same period increased by 62 percent, according to the public health authority.…

stories.headline_en: Kuaishou shares jump after Tencent joins $2.8 billion raise for Kling AI subsidiary

perspectives.headline: Kuaishou shares jump after Tencent joins $2.8 billion raise for Kling AI subsidiary
perspectives.excerpt: Kuaishou Technology shares rose nearly 7% Friday before trimming gains, after the company announced a capital injection of nearly $2.8 billion into its artificial intelligence subsidiary, Kling AI, with backing from tech giant Tencent. The Beijing-based short video platform disclosed the funding details in a regulatory filing released after the market closed on Thursday.…

perspectives.headline: Alibaba, Tencent Join $2.8 Billion Funding for Kling AI
perspectives.excerpt: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. joined a $2.8 billion financing round for Kuaishou Technology’s Kling AI, valuing the creator of one of China’s most popular generative video services at about $15 billion before the investment.

perspectives.headline: Venezuelan man saved from collapsed mall eight days after earthquakes
perspectives.excerpt: A 43-year-old security guard who survived last week’s devastating earthquakes in Venezuela thanks to a pocket of air in his workstation cabin has been pulled from the collapsed basement of a shopping centre amid huge cheers from international rescue teams.…

perspectives.headline: Security guard rescued from rubble 8 days after Venezuela earthquakes
perspectives.excerpt: Security guard rescued from Venezuela earthquake rubble 8 days after massive temblors: "Truly a miracle" Catia La Mar, Venezuela — Hundreds of rescuers in Venezuela cheered and embraced Thursday after pulling a man alive from the ruins of a collapsed building eight days after deadly twin earthquakes, AFP journalists witnessed.…

perspectives.excerpt: South Korea’s won strengthened after a person familiar with the matter said the nation’s officials were preparing for currency flows related to SK Hynix Inc.’s offering of American depositary receipts.

perspectives.headline: Portugal fans celebrate World Cup win over Croatia
perspectives.excerpt: Thousands of Portugal supporters gathered at Lisbon's Terreiro do Paço to watch their national team defeat Croatia 2-1 and book a place in the World Cup last 16. Before kick-off, fans illuminated their phone lights to honour Diogo Jota on the first anniversary of the Portugal forward's death. Cristiano Ronaldo equalised from the penalty spot before Gonçalo Ramos headed in a stoppage-time winner.…

perspectives.excerpt: Ahead of a July 2 deadline to submit public comments, advocates are warning the Federal Trade Commission that it must keep close watch over Elon Musk’s X and firmly reject a recent bid to end the agency’s ongoing audits of the platform’s data handling.…

perspectives.excerpt: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said Wednesday that inflation remains too high and emphasized the central bank's commitment to staying independent and reining in price increases, despite pressure from President Trump to cut interest rates.…

stories.headline_en: How Big Is ‘Love Island USA’? More Than 10 Million People Are Already on Its App

perspectives.excerpt: String bikinis? Check. Extra sunscreen? Check. Walls of cameras so millions of Americans can watch your spicy summer flings happen in real time? Check. Love Island USA, where contestants make out, couple up, and fight while lounging around a neon-colored villa in swimwear, is currently nearing the “climax” of its eighth season.…

perspectives.headline: Why Peacock’s Love Island USA Makes a Perfect Partner for Advertisers
perspectives.excerpt: Peacock’s Love Island USA continues to bring the heat in Season 8, and advertisers are feeling the love. Love Island USA ranked as the No. 1 series on streaming during its first two weeks of Season 8, according to Nielsen. It garnered a combined 2.3 billion minutes viewed during the weeks of June 1 and June 8, up 50% from the previous season’s first two weeks.…

perspectives.headline: Vatican excommunicates rebel SSPX bishops, followers

stories.headline_en: Google loses long-running appeal of record EU fine, will have to cough up $4.7 billion

perspectives.headline: Google loses long-running appeal of record EU fine, will have to cough up $4.7 billion
perspectives.excerpt: Back in 2018, Google was handed a record-setting 4.34 billion-euro ($4.9 billion) fine in Europe for abusing its monopoly on Android. The company has spent the intervening years challenging that decision, but the continent’s highest court has put a stop to that. The Court of Justice of the European Union has affirmed the penalty, meaning Google is out of options.…

perspectives.headline: Google loses final appeal over $4.7 billion EU Android antitrust fine
perspectives.excerpt: Google loses final appeal over $4.7 billion EU Android antitrust fine The decade-long saga is finally at an end, but Google faces further sanctions in the EU. Europe's highest court of appeal has upheld a record-setting €4.1 billion ($4.67 billion) fine imposed on Google back in 2018 for antitrust violations around its Android operating system.…

stories.headline_en: Longer Tesla Model Y L Launches In U.S.

perspectives.headline: Longer Tesla Model Y L Launches In U.S.
perspectives.excerpt: A longer version of Model Y has launched in the U.S. The Model Y L (long wheelbase) three-row, 6-seater has arrived. The AWD L “Launch Series” starts at $61,990 and is now the priciest Model Y in the lineup – more than the performance version, which starts at $57,990.…

perspectives.headline: Tesla's Model Y L finally comes to the US with six seats and a $62,000 price tag
perspectives.excerpt: Tesla's Model Y L finally comes to the US with six seats and a $62,000 price tag Deliveries will begin in September. Tesla has finally launched the Model Y Long Wheelbase in the US and Puerto Rico and has started taking orders for the variant, almost a year after the company released it in China.…
